task_completion.description: The OpenBCI GUI is launched and the correct playback file is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible. In the system control panel, 'Playback' mode is selected and the file 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is loaded as the data source.

task_completion.description: EEG data from the playback file is actively streaming in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Time series graphs showing EEG waveforms are visible and updating in the OpenBCI GUI. The 'Start Data Stream' button has been clicked (typically changes to 'Stop Data Stream').

task_completion.description: A recording session is initiated and runs for at least 5 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI shows an active recording indicator or timer that reaches at least 00:00:05, or a widget indicating that a recording of sufficient length was captured.

task_completion.description: The resulting recording file is renamed to 'alpha_calibration.txt' in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the directory '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/' containing a file explicitly named 'alpha_calibration.txt'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenBCI GUI to generate the recording rather than faking it via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenBCI GUI interface actively streaming and recording. The output file should not be created solely via terminal commands like 'cp' or 'echo'.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create or edit the contents of the output file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (e.g., nano, vim, gedit) should be seen open with the contents of 'alpha_calibration.txt' being manually typed or pasted.

integrity.description: The agent renamed the newly generated recording file, not the original source file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file manager or terminal should show the new file 'alpha_calibration.txt' as a distinct file from the source file, ideally with a file size/timestamp consistent with a short 5+ second recording rather than the full original playback file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file must be named 'alpha_calibration.txt' and located in '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/'. The source playback file is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'. A valid 5-second recording at the standard 250 Hz sample rate will contain approximately 1250-1256 lines (including header comments starting with '%'). The output format is OpenBCI-RAW CSV text.
